heading: Property in the Supreme Court Building and grounds
label: 6133 Property in the Supreme Court Building and grounds
text: It is unlawful to step or climb on, remove, or in any way injure any statue, seat, wall, fountain, or other erection or architectural feature, or any tree, shrub, plant, or turf, in the Supreme Court Building or grounds.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)613340:13i., .Aug. 18, 1949, ch. 479, § 463 Stat. 617The word “fountain” conforms to the original text as signed into law by the President. A typographical error was made in printing the source law in the Statutes-at-Large () where the word appears as “foundation”.63 Stat. 617
